name: Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón – circular route via the Bustalveinte glacier route (PR-S 66)
name_original: Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón – circular por la ruta del glaciar de Bustalveinte (PR-S 66)
region_intro: Bus station hiking trails in Collados Del Asón traverse a landscape shaped by karstic formations and glacial activity, featuring impressive limestone outcrops and deeply eroded river valleys. The region, nestled in the Cantabrian Mountains, offers diverse terrain from beech and oak forests to high-altitude plateaus. Altitudes range significantly, providing varied hiking experiences across its 4,740 hectares. Best bus station hiking trails in Collados Del Asón The most popular bus station hiking route is Grazing area – Plaza de Las Machorras loop from Las Machorras, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 54 minutes to complete, offering views of the characteristic Cantabrian interior. Another top favourite among local hikers is Plaza de Las Machorras – Grazing area loop from Las Machorras, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.6 km) path. This route explores similar pastoral landscapes and takes approximately 3 hours 15 minutes. Local hikers also love the Pico Porracolina via Calseca Stream Trail, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain towards a significant peak, often completed in about 4 hours 58 minutes. Hiking in Collados Del Asón is defined by karstic formations, limestone outcrops, and diverse forests, including beech and oak groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Collados Del Asón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 1,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Collados Del Asón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many bus station hiking trails are available in Collados Del Asón?
A: There are 6 dedicated hiking trails in Collados Del Asón that are easily accessible from bus stations, offering a convenient way to explore the natural park without a car.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on bus station hikes in Collados Del Asón?
A: The bus station hikes in Collados Del Asón range from moderate to difficult. You'll encounter varied terrain, including karstic formations, limestone outcrops, and forested paths. For example, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991902" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grazing area – Plaza de Las Machorras loop from Las Machorras&lt;/a&gt; are moderate, while the ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996940056/pico-porracolina-via-calseca-stream-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Porracolina via Calseca Stream Trail&lt;/a&gt; presents a more challenging experience with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes accessible from bus stops?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. You can enjoy loops such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991902" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grazing area – Plaza de Las Machorras loop from Las Machorras&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from La Pedrosa&lt;/a&gt;, which bring you back to your starting point near a bus stop.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see along these trails?
A: Many trails offer stunning natural features. You can discover the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Asón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4338843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Honda&lt;/a&gt; gorge, or enjoy panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4347749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Posadía&lt;/a&gt;. The Brenarromán Plateau also offers wonderful views of the Cantabrian interior landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Collados Del Asón?
A: Collados del Asón is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn are particularly recommended. Spring brings lush greenery and blooming wildflowers, while autumn transforms the beech and oak forests into a spectacle of vibrant colors. Summer is also popular, but be prepared for warmer temperatures. Winter hikes are possible but may require appropriate gear for snow and ice, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are these bus station trails suitable for families?
A: While some routes in the park are family-friendly, the bus station trails listed here are generally moderate to difficult. For families with younger children, it's advisable to check the specific route details for distance and elevation gain. The park's Interpretation Center in La Gándara de Soba can provide more information on easier options within the wider park area.

Q: Can I bring my dog on these hikes?
A: Collados del Asón Natural Park generally welcomes dogs, but they should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always check specific trail regulations or local signage, especially in protected areas or during nesting seasons.

Q: What are the typical lengths of hikes accessible from bus stops?
A: The bus station hikes in this guide typically range from approximately 9.6 kilometers to 15.9 kilometers. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31855564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Las Machorras – Grazing area loop from Las Machorras&lt;/a&gt; is about 10.5 kilometers, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Porracolina – Pico Porracolina loop from La Pedrosa&lt;/a&gt; is closer to 16 kilometers.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Collados Del Asón?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 220 ratings. Reviewers often praise the tranquil and authentic scenery, the impressive natural features like the Asón Waterfall, and the diverse landscapes shaped by karstic and glacial processes.

Q: Is there an information center for the Collados Del Asón Natural Park?
A: Yes, visitors can find valuable information, maps, and insights into the park's geomorphology, vegetation, fauna, and cultural activities at the Collados del Asón Natural Park Interpretation Center, located in La Gándara de Soba.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I spot while hiking?
A: Collados del Asón is home to abundant wildlife. Keep an eye out for various raptors soaring overhead and different species of bats. The park's diverse ecosystems, including unique Cantabrian oak groves and small beech forests, support a rich array of fauna.
